title: arctous alpina var. japonica
intro: Arctous alpina var. japonica, also known as the Japanese bearberry, is a deciduous shrub native to mountainous regions of East Asia. It is a type of arbutus plant, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and antiseptic qualities, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and urinary tract infections. The plant's leaves and berries have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and berries of Arctous alpina var. japonica are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness their medicinal properties.
